name: Pakistan
summary: Day 49. MUNIR TEHRAN SHUTTLE COMPLETED — Pakistan Army Chief Field Marshal Asim Munir led delegation (Interior Minister Naqvi, FM officials, security/technical experts) to Tehran April 15 to deliver US message to FM Araghchi and prepare framework for Round 2. First CONFIRMED bilateral US-proxy contact between Washington's channel and Tehran since Islamabad collapsed April 12. Araghchi's post-meeting 'commitment to promoting peace and stability in the region remains firm' is most conciliatory FM-level Iranian language since April 8. White House April 15 via Leavitt: Round 2 'very likely' at same Islamabad venue. Shuttle format is classic post-collapse revival pattern that worked for April 6-8 ceasefire. No date confirmed; AP sources suggest April 17-20 window. Pakistan's mediator role now extends to shuttle diplomacy — Munir carrying US message to Iran, delegation-level bilateral statements. Pakistan's dual role intact: mediator for US-Iran AND military partner (13,000 troops + fighter jets to Saudi Arabia). 5-6 ceasefire days remain.

signals.content: Pakistan FM Ishaq Dar publicly confirmed 'US-Iran indirect talks are taking place through messages being relayed by Pakistan' — first third-party validation of a communication channel. Turkey and Egypt also helping relay messages. (Sources: NPR, OPB, CNBC)

Pakistan hosted Islamabad quadrilateral meeting — FMs from Saudi Arabia (Prince Faisal bin Farhan), Turkey (Hakan Fidan), Egypt (Badr Abdelatti) met in Islamabad. Turkish FM Fidan sought to 'establish a mechanism aimed at de-escalation.' No joint statement issued. Talks continued March 30. (Sources: Bloomberg, NPR, Al Jazeera, Business Standard, Egyptian Streets)

Pakistan offered to host direct US-Iran talks 'in coming days' — elevating from bilateral message relay to hosting venue for potential face-to-face negotiations. The quad exchanged 'assessments on dire economic repercussions' including supply chains, food security, energy security. Pakistan positioned as neutral mediator — maintains relations with both Iran (shared border, Balochistan) and US (military/intelligence cooperation). (Sources: Bloomberg, NPR, Time, Al Jazeera)

Pakistan emerging as key diplomatic player. Committee of Four (Pakistan, Turkey, Egypt, Saudi Arabia) created formal diplomatic mechanism in Islamabad March 29. Deputy PM Dar confirmed Pakistan hosting 'direct US-Iran talks in coming days' — most specific commitment to date. Iran has NOT confirmed attendance. Pakistan's Hormuz deal executing: MV Multan (cargo) and P-Akili (crude) crossing, expected at Karachi March 31. 20-ship deal (2/day). Pakistan contradicted Trump's '20 boats as sign of respect' — says they're PAKISTANI ships under bilateral deal. CONFIRMED.

Pakistan became first mediator to publicly confirm on record that 'US-Iran indirect talks are taking place' — a Rubio-Araghchi meeting in Pakistan could happen 'within days.' This is the most significant diplomatic development: moving from anonymous sourcing to on-record confirmation of mediation. Combined with Iran's Pezeshkian telling EU he has 'necessary will' to end the conflict, the diplomatic track has the most substance since the war began — even as both sides publicly deny talks. (Sources: Pakistan FM, Bloomberg, Press TV)

Diplomatic sources cited by Pakistan's Dawn newspaper suggested a Rubio-Araghchi meeting 'as early as Tuesday' (April 1) — the meeting did NOT materialize. Iran did NOT participate in the Islamabad four-nation meeting. FM Araghchi stated Iran was prepared for 'at least six months' of war while simultaneously listing end conditions — dealmaking language suggesting Iran wants to negotiate from a position of demonstrated endurance. The diplomatic window suggested by sources appears closed without a face-to-face meeting materializing. Significant because the April 6 deadline is now 4 days away with no visible diplomatic progress. CONFIRMED. (Sources: Anadolu Agency, Al Jazeera, Time)

Rubio-Araghchi meeting still hasn't materialized — 'within days' for over a week now. Pezeshkian told Pakistani PM he was attacked twice during previous nuclear talks — deepening skepticism. Demands confidence-building measures (strike pause) before direct talks = circular dependency with US position. China positioning as potential peace broker per CNN — could bypass Pakistan channel. Pakistan still 'preparing' but credibility of mediation timeline eroding. (Sources: Dawn, Al Jazeera, The Hill, CNN)

Pakistan named as one of three mediators (with Egypt and Turkey) in the 45-day ceasefire framework reported by Axios. This is a two-phase deal: Phase 1 is a 45-day ceasefire during which a permanent end to war is negotiated (extendable), Phase 2 is permanent agreement. Witkoff texting Araghchi directly — bypassing formal diplomatic channels. Pakistan's mediation role is now formalized in a named framework, upgrading from informal message relay to structured diplomatic participant. Previous Pakistan-led ceasefire talks had 'hit a dead end' (WSJ/Al Arabiya April 3). Mediators assess chances as 'slim' but call this 'the only chance to prevent a dramatic escalation' including massive strikes on Iranian civilian infrastructure and retaliation against Gulf energy/water. Iran's sticking points: will not fully give up Hormuz reopening or highly enriched uranium stockpile for only 45 days of ceasefire. All US proposals rejected so far. (Sources: Axios, Al-Monitor, TRT World, Al Arabiya, Arms Control Association)

PAKISTAN-BROKERED ISLAMABAD ACCORD COLLAPSED [CONFIRMED]. Pakistan's Field Marshal Asim Munir was reportedly 'in contact all night long' with VP JD Vance, Witkoff, and Araghchi. The proposal — tentatively dubbed the 'Islamabad Accord' — would have triggered an immediate ceasefire reopening Hormuz with 15-20 days for a broader settlement (and an underlying 45-day phase). Tehran rejected the temporary nature, transmitting a formal 10-point counter via Pakistan instead. Despite the collapse of THIS proposal, the back-channel through Pakistan remains live — Iran transmitted its counter formally through Pakistan rather than rejecting outright. This is Pakistan's biggest geopolitical moment in years: Asim Munir is being positioned as a regional power broker, prominently elevated in Pakistani media (Pakistan Today, Express Tribune). Two Qatar LNG tankers were also quietly allowed through Hormuz under a Pakistan-brokered secret deal, per Business Upturn reporting.

PAKISTAN BROKERS US-IRAN 2-WEEK CEASEFIRE — BIGGEST GEOPOLITICAL MOMENT SINCE 2001 [CONFIRMED]. Pakistani PM Shehbaz Sharif spent April 7 brokering the 2-week ceasefire framework after the original 45-day Islamabad Accord collapsed Sunday-Monday on Iran's 10-point counter. With less than two hours left before the 8 PM ET deadline, Sharif appealed directly to Trump to extend by two weeks. Trump posted suspension on Truth Social ~2 hours later, conditional on Iran agreeing to Hormuz 'COMPLETE OPENING.' Iran's SNSC accepted, explicitly stating 'the Pakistani prime minister has informed Iran that the United States has accepted Iran's 10-point plan as the basis for negotiations.' Talks open Friday April 10 in Islamabad. Field Marshal Asim Munir and Sharif emerge as the primary mediators of the war. This is Pakistan's biggest geopolitical moment in years — possibly since 2001. Pakistan now sits at the centre of the Hormuz, US-Iran, China-Iran, and Saudi-Iran diplomatic axes simultaneously.

Pakistan PM Sharif contradicted both Trump and Netanyahu on ceasefire scope, stating it covers 'everywhere including Lebanon' — creating a three-way contradiction that is the structural fault line for Friday April 10 Islamabad talks. Trump called Lebanon a 'separate skirmish'; Netanyahu explicitly excluded Lebanon; Iran's 10-point plan includes Lebanon per Sharif's reading. Pakistan media running zero-skepticism triumphalist coverage: 'Pakistan saves a whole civilisation.' Friday Islamabad talks are the next binary event — success extends ceasefire, failure collapses it. (Source: NBC News, CBS News, Express Tribune, Pakistan Today)

Day 43. ISLAMABAD HOSTING DAY. Preparatory diplomatic activity Friday April 10. Formal talks Saturday April 11 at Serena Hotel. Islamabad declared public holiday. PM Sharif hosting — thanked Gulf nations for 'commitment to peace and stability.' US delegation: VP Vance (lead), Witkoff, Kushner. Iran delegation: Ghalibaf (lead), Araghchi. Three red lines already drawn: enrichment, Lebanon scope, Hormuz sovereignty. Sharif continues to insist ceasefire covers 'everywhere including Lebanon' — contradicting both Trump and Netanyahu. Russia-China vetoed Bahrain's UNSC Hormuz resolution (April 7, 11-2 vote) — closing multilateral pathway, making Pakistan-mediated bilateral the only avenue. Saudi-Iranian FM first call since war occurred ahead of Islamabad. (Source: Al Jazeera, Bloomberg, CNN, Axios, PBS, UN News)

Day 44. ISLAMABAD TALKS BEGIN — PROXIMITY FORMAT [CONFIRMED ACTION + RHETORIC on preconditions]. Both delegations arrived at Serena Hotel. US: VP JD Vance (lead — first time since 1979 hostage crisis a US VP has negotiated with Iranians; historical significance), Steve Witkoff, Jared Kushner plus 30-member advance security team. Iran: Parliament Speaker Ghalibaf (lead), FM Araghchi, SNSC Secretary Ali-Akbar Ahmadian, Central Bank Governor Hemmati + several MPs — arrived late Thursday night at Nur Khan Airbase. Format: PROXIMITY TALKS — both teams at Serena Hotel but in SEPARATE ROOMS, Pakistani officials shuttling messages. They will NOT sit face-to-face. Pakistan requisitioned the entire Serena Hotel on April 8, evacuated all guests, declared a two-day public holiday, and established a 3-kilometer red zone. Pakistani fighter jets escorted the Iranian delegation's plane. Ghalibaf set two PRECONDITIONS before negotiations begin: (1) ceasefire in Lebanon, (2) release of Iran's blocked assets — 'These two matters must be fulfilled before negotiations begin.' Ghalibaf: 'We have goodwill, but we do not have trust.' Cited three alleged ceasefire violations: continued Israeli Lebanon strikes, drone in Iranian airspace, denial of enrichment rights. Trump: Iran has 'no cards,' flatly rejected enrichment: 'There will be no enrichment of Uranium.' Vance: warned Iran 'not to play us.' Pakistan's explicit MODEST goal: 'just enough common ground to keep talks going,' not a comprehensive deal. Negotiating basis: Iran's 10-point proposal vs US 15-point proposal. Trump separately told an Israeli journalist he instructed Netanyahu to keep operations 'low key' to avoid derailing Pakistan talks — a shift from 'Lebanon not included' to implicit acknowledgment that Lebanon violence threatens the talks. Note: the trigger 'Pakistan hosts direct US-Iran face-to-face talks' is NOT activated because the format is explicitly PROXIMITY (separate rooms), not face-to-face. (Sources: Al Jazeera liveblog, PressTV, PBS, Axios, Asharq Al-Awsat, NBC News, CBS News)

Islamabad Talks ran 21 hours across April 11-12 and ended without agreement — the most significant diplomatic event Pakistan has hosted since the 1999 Lahore Declaration. PM Shehbaz Sharif met VP Vance and Speaker Ghalibaf separately. FM Ishaq Dar called the 21-hour session 'intense and constructive' and urged both sides to maintain the ceasefire. Pakistan facilitated the format evolution from proximity talks to trilateral face-to-face — the first US-Iran principals in the same room since 1979. Despite the no-deal, Pakistan's mediator role is validated: Wilson Center's Kugelman said Pakistan 'defied the skeptics.' Saudi/Chinese/Egyptian/Qatari officials were present as indirect facilitators, but Pakistan ran the process. Three paths forward per Kugelman: low-level technical discussions, future summits possibly in Muscat or Doha, or 'maximum pressure' period before next round. (Sources: CNBC, Al Jazeera, CBS News, Tribune India, Wilson Center)

Pakistan sent 13,000 troops and fighter jets to Saudi Arabia as a post-ceasefire security commitment (CONFIRMED — source: Al Jazeera April 12, CS Monitor April 1). Facilitating potential Thursday April 16 talks via the 'Islamabad Process' — AP reports via Middle East Eye and NDTV that another round could be held 'as early as Thursday,' with Pakistan, Turkey, and Egypt facilitating. Two venues under consideration. This is the closest signal to renewed diplomacy after Islamabad failure but comes from mediators and unnamed officials, not confirmed by either principal (source: AP via Middle East Eye, NDTV April 14). Pakistan's dual role now explicit: mediator for US-Iran diplomacy AND military partner providing security to Saudi Arabia.

Day 48. SECOND ROUND TALKS ACTIVELY BEING ORGANIZED — MULTI-SOURCE CONFIRMED [CONFIRMED under discussion, NOT yet scheduled]. Full source constellation: (1) AP/Reuters citing 5 sources: US and Iranian teams returning to Islamabad this week, delegations keeping Friday-Sunday (April 17-19) open. (2) White House official told TIME: 'Future talks are under discussion but nothing has been scheduled at this time.' (3) CNN independently reported April 13: 'US eyes potential second round of in-person talks with Iran as blockade takes hold.' (4) CNBC April 14: White House confirmed discussions. (5) A mediating country diplomat told reporters both sides 'agreed to more talks' but location, timing, and delegation composition not decided — Islamabad and Geneva both under consideration. (6) Pakistan proactively offered to host Round 2 'even before the ceasefire ended' (AP). (7) PM Sharif heading to Riyadh and Ankara for shuttle diplomacy — Al Jazeera framed as 'amid prospect of new US-Iran talks.' (8) A source involved in the first talks said they were '80% there' before hitting decisions requiring principal-level authority. (9) Pakistan Today (April 15) reported 'US-Iran set for possible second round of Islamabad talks this week.' Iran's FM Araghchi posted on X that Iran was 'inches away from Islamabad MoU' before encountering 'maximalism' — blame-framing that preserves, not closes, the door. Iran has NOT publicly rejected a second round. The Friday-Sunday window is effectively the LAST opportunity before ceasefire expires April 21. Pakistan's mediator role now extends beyond hosting to active shuttle diplomacy (Sharif to Riyadh/Ankara).

CONFIRMED ACTION: Field Marshal Asim Munir led Pakistani delegation — including Interior Minister Mohsin Naqvi, Foreign Ministry officials, and security/technical experts — to Tehran on April 15 to deliver a US message to Araghchi and prepare the framework for a second round of talks. Araghchi received the delegation; press TV confirmed meeting; Bloomberg/Al-Monitor/PBS/Tribune/Asianet/Geo TV/Middle East Monitor corroborated. Araghchi publicly thanked Pakistan for hosting first round and stated Iran's 'commitment to promoting peace and stability in the region remains firm' — most conciliatory FM-level Iranian language since April 8 SNSC ceasefire acceptance. White House confirmed April 15 via Leavitt that next round of US-Iran talks 'very likely' at same Islamabad venue. This is the FIRST CONFIRMED bilateral contact between Washington's proxy (Pakistan) and Tehran since Islamabad collapsed April 12, and directly contradicts Vance's April 12 'final and best offer' framing. Shuttle pattern (mediator carrying message from side A to side B, returning) is classic post-collapse revival format — worked for the Islamabad Accord April 6-8 and is now being re-run. Munir's visit is the OPERATIONAL EVIDENCE that Trump's April 14-15 'close to over' posture shift has substance. (Sources: Press TV, Bloomberg, Al-Monitor, PBS, Tribune India, Geo TV, Middle East Monitor)

edges.context: Day 49. Munir Tehran shuttle April 15 — CONFIRMED ACTION. Delivered US message to Araghchi. Araghchi's 'commitment to peace and stability' statement most conciliatory FM-level language since April 8. Pakistan's mediator role now operationalized as physical shuttle, not just message relay. Round 2 venue 'very likely' Islamabad per Leavitt.

Day 49. Munir carrying US message to Tehran — CONFIRMED shuttle format resumed post-Islamabad collapse. White House via Leavitt confirmed Round 2 'very likely' Islamabad venue. Pakistan's Sharif continues Riyadh/Ankara shuttle diplomacy. Shuttle pattern is same post-collapse revival format that worked April 6-8.
Pakistan sent 13,000 troops + fighter jets to Saudi Arabia (CONFIRMED). Saudi FM attended Islamabad quad. Upgraded from diplomatic coordination to active military partnership.
Pakistan's mediation directly affects oil prices — the Islamabad quad + hosting offer contributed to Brent's -4.3% pullback on March 30.

Pakistan's Hormuz deal executing: 20-ship deal (2/day). MV Multan (cargo) and P-Akili (crude) crossing, expected Karachi March 31. Pakistan contradicted Trump — says PAKISTANI ships under bilateral deal, not '20 boats as sign of respect.'

trigger_points.condition: Pakistan hosts direct US-Iran face-to-face talks
trigger_points.mechanism: Direct talks would be the biggest de-escalation signal of the war. Oil could drop 10-15% in a session. Rupee rally. Nifty surge. But talks ≠ deal — the gap between positions remains enormous.
